violations.explanation: Prohibition 7 violation: Function-level docstring found inside the function definition. The docstring should be at MODULE level (before imports), not inside the function. Framework standard requires module-level docstring only.

fix_details.description: Move docstring from inside function to module level (before imports)

violations.explanation: Missing .elements. namespace when accessing locator. According to the page additions file, print_history_card is defined as a string selector in the Elements class, so it must be accessed via .elements. namespace. Direct property access (print_history_page.print_history_card) is only valid if the locator is defined as self.print_history_card = page.locator(...) in __init__. The page additions file shows it should be in Elements class.
Missing .elements. namespace. The page additions file defines complimentary_pages_progress_bar in the Elements class as a string selector, requiring .elements. access pattern.
Missing .elements. namespace for tooltip locator access.
Missing .elements. namespace for info message locator access.
Missing .elements. namespace for additional pages progress bar access.
Missing .elements. namespace and incorrect usage pattern. When accessing Elements class selectors for JavaScript evaluation, must use page.locator(page_obj.elements.selector).evaluate() pattern.

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Add .elements. namespace to all locator accesses in the test script. Every reference to print_history_page.{locator} should be changed to print_history_page.elements.{locator} when the locator is defined in the Elements class. For JavaScript evaluation, use page.locator(page_obj.elements.selector).evaluate() pattern.

Replace method calls to verify_plan_pause_info_not_displayed() and verify_plan_pause_info_displayed() with direct expect() assertions using page.locator(page_obj.elements.locator) pattern, since these methods don't exist in the actual PrintHistoryPage class yet.
Move the docstring from inside the function definition to module level (before all imports). This requires restructuring the file to have: docstring → imports → function definition.

Complete the implementation of precondition 4 (pause plan). Either implement the actual pause action using existing GeminiRAHelper methods or create a new helper method to encapsulate the pause operation. Remove TODO comments and ensure the plan is actually paused before test steps begin.

Restructure the page additions file to properly define locators within an Elements inner class. Wrap all locator string definitions in a class Elements: block to match the framework's page object pattern.
Add module-level docstring at the very top of the file (before imports) with test case ID, title, and description.
Encapsulate the pause plan admin interaction in a single helper method rather than multiple inline GeminiRAHelper calls with TODO comments.
Remove 'from playwright.sync_api import expect' from inside all methods and add it once at module level (top of file).
Enhance the module-level docstring with more detailed merge instructions including warnings about generic selectors and import requirements.
Add a clear separator comment after precondition setup and before Step 1 to distinguish setup from actual test steps.
Complete the implementation of Step 10 by verifying and adding correct parameters to send_rtp_devicestatus() for simulating page count. Remove TODO comment.
Replace all TODO comments with NOTE comments or remove them entirely. TODO comments should not be present in production test scripts.
Simplify verbose comments about generic selectors to concise WARNING prefixed comments.
